title: Lesson 426
transcript: Let's talk about some innovative ways that the industry has come up with to promote the perfect development of software that also includes security as part of that, right? We've been talking at length how security needs to be involved in the process of software development. right from the start and throughout, and certainly throughout the decades that we have been producing code and writing applications and programs, for example, we've come up with innovative ways to try and speed up the applications development process and yet still produce quality code. Of course, what we've been emphasizing forever is that security needs to be part of that process to begin with. There are some newer platforms and methodologies that obviously allow that to happen. In fact, in some cases, force security to be involved so that as you know, we've been saying this many times too, the best type of security is always what you design into something based on requirements. That is not something that can be done after the fact. It needs to be done as part of the entire process itself. All right, so let's look at this. Because the traditional way of creating applications and architectures has always been, for example, to understand the requirements and then we have development teams that build whatever it is that we need. And then once we've built what we think what we wanted in the first place, we put it into production and we start using that system or application. At that point, development is basically finished. Then the operations people take over because we're operating in production, for example. Then of course, quality assurance people. get involved in both of these from the start and throughout. And years ago, somebody realized that, wait a minute, we don't need to separate these processes because if we involve everyone, from the start and throughout then we can all collaborate better we can better align the product to goals and objectives of the organization itself and so there's value obviously in involving operations as part of the development and development as part of the operations And so we look at initiatives like this, not as a phased approach to building something, but collaborating and involving everyone right from the start and throughout. has advantages where we can communicate better we can achieve better collaboration between all team members and of course we can better align with goals and objectives of the organization itself now even though know the exam outline and the industry refers to this as integrated product teams you may know it as this right a lot of people call it devops although i think those two words don't do justice to certainly what security has to focus on. In fact, I've seen countless presentations of where people have talked about this concept here to management and stakeholders and they call it DevOps, but we forget about the most important component of all of this that is basically not even shown here. And that is the security component of this, right? Because when you think about it, if you're going to have integrated product teams that collaborate better and work together better and achieve alignment with goals and objectives better, you need to involve security as well. And in fact, look, you can't do development properly without including security right from the start and throughout. And you can't do operations properly without involving security from the start and throughout. And in fact, quality assurance has to focus on those aspects of things that are focused on security as well. So this is more appropriately called, some people will call it DevSecOps. Although I've heard a lot of people argue, that it should be called SecDevOps because that emphasizes the most important piece of all of this, which is security. And in fact, I've seen presentations where they talk about this concept of DevOps and integrated product teams. And they use a similar diagram to what you see here. But then what they do to illustrate the fact that security needs to be involved in all of this is they draw one big circle around all of this And they call it security because security is the glue that connects all of this together and indeed has to touch on every aspect of DevOps, for example. And that's certainly true. We'll talk a little more about this as we go on. But integrated product teams, as far as the exam and the industry is concerned, needs to involve security. So if you're going to explain what this is and how it involves everybody that collaborate together and we're better aligned with goals and objectives, needs to include security as well. In fact, when it comes to DevOps, They have found better ways to do things, of course, by collaborating, of course, but that includes security as well. If you see here DevSecOps manifesto, these are some of the things that security is focused on. in doing much better. If you look at each of these, although you don't have to memorize any of this for purposes of the exam, we're just trying to emphasize, like the exam will, the importance of including security as part of you know integrated product teams right from the start and throughout it's the glue that puts everything together it's the one component that makes everything work perfectly together and you know when you look at the traditional view of what security has been, especially in software development and technology protection and so on. You know, security has always said no, and they've always imposed and gotten in the way, for example, but that's not what DevSecOps manifesto specifically related to security emphasizes it obviously emphasizes really good things like for example making sure that we focus on goals and objectives as opposed to just saying no all the time and We don't mandate security controls, but we look at those that are actually required based on what we're trying to achieve and the value of what these things are trying to produce for us. Having, for example, business driven security over, you know, typical security that we just rubber stamp and impose, for example, and, you know, sharing threat intelligence on everybody involved, development and operations and quality assurance and management and stakeholders. rather than keeping it to ourselves because it's something that we think is valuable only to us. That's the message that we're trying to show here. But obviously, DevOps promotes automation, continuous integration, delivery, all of those things that obviously is really important to be able to deliver valuable products to the organization. The whole purpose of DevOps is to promote automation, continuous integration, and delivery. Of course, here is where we want to get to. and that is to involve security in all of what you see here right from the start and throughout.
